title: Deployments, ReplicaSets, Rollouts, Strategies, Conditions, and Recovery · Mastery Check
summary: A release review compares fresh deployments, replicasets, rollouts, strategies, conditions, and recovery choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace Deployment template hash, ReplicaSet ownership, desired replicas, Pod replacement, adoption boundaries, and observed status.
Calculate RollingUpdate maxSurge, maxUnavailable, total capacity, readiness, termination, and rollout duration under a changed replica count.
Bind Pod template changes to immutable image digests, configuration identity, pull policy, provenance, and selected platform evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership
steps.instruction:
Order the K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership orchestration evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership.
steps.why: Correct order keeps K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership, keep K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz Deployment rolls out image tag latest, stalls at 4 unavailable replicas, exceeds progress deadline, and has a bad ConfigMap change outside revision history. Bind immutable identity, strategy, conditions, pause, rollback, and verification. Competency probe: Trace Deployment template hash, ReplicaSet ownership, desired replicas, Pod replacement, adoption boundaries, and observed status.

stimulus.title: K8s Rollback Causal Recovery failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: kubectl rollout undo reverses every application, configuration, database, and external side effect.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose roll forward or rollback from causal evidence while coordinating schemas, config, traffic, durable state, compatibility, and post-recovery verification.

This does not satisfy K8s Rollback Causal Recovery for observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan.
Run the K8s Rollback Causal Recovery boundary case for observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the K8s Rollback Causal Recovery result because visible observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for K8s Rollback Causal Recovery.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose roll forward or rollback from causal evidence while coordinating schemas, config, traffic, durable state, compatibility, and post-recovery verification.
